summary: Iter22 (BUG burger menu — EnableNotificationsButton coupé sur Android) fully validated. Code review + Playwright mobile (Pixel 7 412x915 + iPhone SE 375x600 + iOS UA 390x844) confirms fix on both DriverDashboard.js and UserDashboard.js: (1) side-menu motion.div has 'overflow-y-auto' class; (2) inner content div has 'pb-12'; (3) <EnableNotificationsButton/> moved to the TOP of the burger menu inside a border-bottom block right after the avatar/name; (4) only ONE occurrence per file. Mobile Pixel 7: notification element rendered at y=161/177 (well within viewport 915px) for both Driver and User dashboards — visible without scrolling. iPhone SE 375x600 (very small viewport): menu correctly becomes scrollable (scrollHeight=738 > clientHeight=600), the EnableNotificationsButton stays visible at top (y=161) without scroll, and the logout button (initially off-screen at y=654) becomes accessible at y=516 after scrolling. Non-regression iOS UA non-standalone: ios-install-pwa-notice rendered (✅), enable-notifications-btn NOT rendered (✅) — component logic preserved.
critical_code_review_comments: DriverDashboard.js:592 — menu container className correctly contains 'overflow-y-auto'; inner div line 594 contains 'pb-12 pt-20' for top respiration (header) and bottom respiration (logout). Single EnableNotificationsButton at line 607 in its own border-b block right after avatar.
UserDashboard.js:649 — identical structure: 'overflow-y-auto' on container, 'pb-12 pt-20' on inner div, single EnableNotificationsButton at line 664 right after avatar, no duplicate.

EnableNotificationsButton.jsx — untouched and behavior intact: returns one of 4 testids ('enable-notifications-btn' | 'notifications-enabled-badge' | 'notifications-denied-badge' | 'ios-install-pwa-notice') based on Notification.permission + iOS standalone detection. In headless Chromium, default Notification.permission='denied' which is why tests observed the denied-badge instead of the active button — this is expected and the user on a real Android device with default 'default' permission will see the actual 'Activer les notifications' yellow button.

Non-blocking observation: NotificationCenter is also rendered in the header before the burger toggle (UserDashboard.js:629). On Pixel 7 this is fine (412px wide); on extremely narrow viewports (<360px) the header may run out of space, but this was outside iter22 scope.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/_seed_iter22.py (new — seed helper for test driver/user + JWTs, with --cleanup option)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 bugs validated: code review DriverDash, code review UserDash, Pixel 7 Driver visibility, Pixel 7 User visibility, iPhone SE scroll behavior, iOS UA non-regression)

test_credentials: JWT signed with JWT_SECRET from /app/backend/.env. Seed driver id=test-iter22-driver, user id=test-iter22-user. Tokens generated by /app/backend/tests/_seed_iter22.py. Seed data cleaned up after test run (verified).
seed_data_creation: Seeded one driver (test-iter22-driver) and one user (test-iter22-user) with is_validated/email_verified=true and active subscription so /auth/me returns 200. Cleanup completed post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable JWT-injection-into-localStorage pattern: localStorage.setItem('token', <HS256 jwt {user_id,role,exp} signed with JWT_SECRET>) then navigate to /driver or /dashboard. Driver dashboard route is /driver (NOT /driver/dashboard). User dashboard route is /dashboard. Burger menu data-testid: driver-menu-toggle (driver) and menu-toggle-btn (user). Always grant geolocation + setGeolocation({lat,lng}) before /driver else map shows GPS error screen. Dismiss the 'Important' subscription popup with button:has-text("J'ai compris") on user dashboard. In Chromium headless Notification.permission defaults to 'denied', so EnableNotificationsButton renders 'notifications-denied-badge' instead of 'enable-notifications-btn' — both are valid evidence of the component being mounted.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: Capitaine's Android screenshot showed missing 'Activer les notifications' button because the burger menu container (motion.div h-full w-72) had NO overflow-y-auto and its content (avatar + 5 info cards + 4 action buttons + notification button) exceeded the device viewport height. On Pixel 7 + Android Chrome, the content overflowed the bottom edge of the viewport with no scroll mechanism, hiding the bottom-most buttons (Changer mot de passe, Activer notifications, Logout). FIX: (a) added 'overflow-y-auto' on the motion.div container of BOTH DriverDashboard.js (l.592) and UserDashboard.js (l.649); (b) added 'pb-12' on the inner content div for breathing room; (c) repositioned <EnableNotificationsButton/> from bottom-of-menu to top-of-menu (in a border-bottom block right after the avatar block) so even on the narrowest devices (iPhone SE 375x600 confirmed) the notification button is visible without scrolling. Validation confirms: notif at y=161 (visible without scroll on a 600px tall viewport), and menu remains fully scrollable so logout button at y=654 becomes reachable at y=516 after scrolling. No regression on EnableNotificationsButton component itself.
